title: LLMReconciliationWindow
description: Window-close event emitted by apps/worker/reconciler when a (tenant, provider, model, window_start) bucket closes — carries the per-window estimated vs. reconciled cost and the trivial drift ratio. Drift math is intentionally trivial: drift_usd = reconciled_cost_usd - estimated_cost_usd; drift_ratio = drift_usd / max(estimated_cost_usd, epsilon). Decision logic on the drift (alerting threshold, routing action, budget enforcement) is outside this event contract.

event_id.description: Deterministic identifier derived from the unique key of the reconciliation row plus its terminal status. Replaying a window close produces a byte-identical event (idempotent consumer guarantee).

window_start.description: RFC3339 timestamp of the window's start (truncated to the configured window size).

window_end.description: RFC3339 timestamp of the window's end.

estimated_cost_usd.description: Sum of runtime cost estimates contributed to this window (USD float; the reconciler stores integer minor units in Postgres and converts at the bus boundary).

reconciled_cost_usd.description: Vendor-reconciled cost share allocated to this window from llm.usage.reconciled events covering the period.

drift_ratio.description: drift_usd divided by max(reconciled_cost_usd, epsilon). Trivial arithmetic — no scoring, no weighting.

status.description: Terminal status of the window. 'reconciled' means both sides contributed non-zero cost; 'unreconciled' means only one side did within the grace period.
